description.en: Wales's beloved teatime loaf: raisins, sultanas and currants steeped overnight in strong tea, then folded into a spiced batter and baked low and slow. No kneading required, just sliced thin, buttered, and served for St David's Day or Christmas.
description.de: Waliens beliebtes Teebrot: Rosinen, Sultaninen und Korinthen ziehen über Nacht in starkem Tee, werden mit Gewürzen zu einem Teig verrührt und langsam gebacken. Kein Kneten nötig – dünn geschnitten und gebuttert zum St. David's Day oder Weihnachten.
description.es: El pan de té favorito de Gales: pasas, sultanas y grosellas se remojan toda la noche en té negro fuerte, se mezclan con especias y se hornean despacio. Sin amasado, se sirve en rebanadas finas con mantequilla para el Día de San David o Navidad.
description.fr: Le pain au thé préféré du pays de Galles : raisins secs et groseilles trempent toute la nuit dans un thé noir fort, puis sont mêlés à une pâte épicée et cuits lentement. Sans pétrissage, tranché fin et beurré, pour la Saint David ou Noël.

origin.note: Two real traditions exist: an older yeast-leavened bread and this now-dominant chemically-leavened tea loaf. This entry follows the tea loaf, the version most Welsh households and cafés make today; eaten at teatime, St David's Day and Christmas.

ingredients.note: poured hot over the fruit, soaked overnight

keeping.note: Tea-soaked fruit keeps this loaf moist far longer than most quick breads; many say it's best on day two or three.

method.title: Soak the fruit
method.summary: Pour hot, strong tea over the mixed dried fruit, cover, and leave overnight (or at least 6 hours) until plump.

method.title: Mix the batter
method.summary: Stir the self-raising flour, muscovado sugar and mixed spice together, then fold in the soaked fruit with its liquid and the beaten egg until just combined.

method.title: Pan it up
method.summary: Scrape the thick batter into a lined loaf tin and level the top.

method.title: Bake low and slow
method.summary: Bake at 180°C for about an hour, loosely covering if the top browns too quickly, until a skewer comes out clean.

method.title: Glaze and rest
method.summary: Brush the warm loaf with honey, cool completely, then wrap and let it sit a day or two for the best flavor before slicing.

source.adaptation: Picked the dominant chemical tea-loaf version over the older yeasted original. Hydration% treats soaking tea as dough liquid, adapted since it's absorbed by fruit, not kneaded into gluten.
